name: Mahora from Albacete — circular by Valdeganga
name_original: Mahora desde Albacete — circular por Valdeganga
description: Cycle the moderate 42.9-mile Mahora from Albacete — circular by Valdeganga route, featuring a dedicated bike lane and scenic views.
region_intro: Road cycling around Centro offers diverse terrain, from the challenging climbs of the Serra da Estrela mountain range to the rolling hills and flat coastal paths of the Silver Coast. The region features river valleys, such as the Ecopista do Dão, which provides paved routes along former railway lines. Karstic formations and varied natural parks contribute to a landscape suitable for different cycling preferences. Best road cycling routes around Centro The most popular road cycling route is Chinchilla de Monte-Aragón — circular desde Albacete, a 45.4 miles (73.1 km) trail that takes 3 hours 3 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers varied terrain for road cyclists. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Mahora from Albacete — circular by Valdeganga, a moderate 42.9 miles (69.1 km) path. This route features rolling landscapes and river views. Local road cyclists also love the Peñas de San Pedro y Pozohondo — circular desde Albacete, a 59.6 miles (95.9 km) trail leading through varied countryside, often completed in about 4 hours 14 minutes. Road cycling around Centro is defined by mountainous terrain, rolling hills, and flat coastal routes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy rides to more challenging ascents. The routes in Centro are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 2,700 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Centro's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes can I find in the Centro region?
A: The Centro region offers a wide variety of road cycling routes, with over 60 routes available on komoot. These routes cater to all levels, from easy coastal rides to challenging mountain ascents.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Centro?
A: Yes, Centro has routes perfect for beginners. There are 12 easy routes available, often featuring flatter terrain like the coastal paths along the Silver Coast or converted railway lines such as the Ecopista do Dão. A great option to start with is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1154791501/santa-ana-from-albacete-circular-via-aguas-nuevas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Ana from Albacete — circular via Aguas Nuevas&lt;/a&gt;, which is an easy ride.

Q: Where can I find challenging road cycling routes with significant elevation gain in Centro?
A: For advanced cyclists seeking a challenge, Centro offers 11 difficult routes, particularly in the mountainous terrain of the Serra da Estrela. These routes feature significant elevation gains and winding paths through breathtaking landscapes. An example of a more challenging route is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1167753118/bogarra-circular-from-penas-de-san-pedro" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bogarra — circular from Peñas de San Pedro&lt;/a&gt;, which includes over 1,500 meters of elevation gain.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in Centro?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes in Centro are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. For instance, the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1134081277/chinchilla-de-monte-aragon-circular-desde-albacete" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chinchilla de Monte-Aragón — circular desde Albacete&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate circular route, as is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1134633953/mahora-from-albacete-circular-by-valdeganga" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mahora from Albacete — circular by Valdeganga&lt;/a&gt;, offering varied terrain and scenic views.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while road cycling in Centro?
A: Centro's landscapes are incredibly diverse. You'll encounter challenging mountainous terrain in the Serra da Estrela, rolling hills and backcountry roads, and incredibly flat, scenic coastal routes along the Silver Coast. The region also features river valleys, such as the Dão River, and unique karstic formations in areas like the Natural Park of Aire and Candeeiros Mountains.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about the routes in Centro?
A: The road cycling routes in Centro are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 10 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, from challenging ascents to peaceful river paths, and the opportunity to explore charming villages and natural parks.

Q: Are there any notable attractions or landmarks along the road cycling routes in Centro?
A: Many routes pass by interesting attractions. You might cycle past historical sites like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/981788" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chinchilla Castle&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5573464" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cathedral of Saint John the Baptist of Albacete&lt;/a&gt;. Natural features include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5622681" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna Salada de Pétrola&lt;/a&gt; and the serene &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5738064" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Abelardo Sánchez Park&lt;/a&gt;. The region also boasts unique geological formations and picturesque river valleys.

Q: Can I find routes that offer scenic viewpoints in Centro?
A: Absolutely. The diverse topography of Centro means many routes offer stunning scenic viewpoints. From the high mountain passes of Serra da Estrela to the coastal roads overlooking the Atlantic, you'll find numerous spots to take in the breathtaking scenery. Routes through river valleys and natural parks also provide picturesque vistas.

Q: Are there any dedicated cycle paths or greenways for road cycling in Centro?
A: Yes, the region features excellent dedicated cycle paths. A prime example is the Ecopista do Dão, a 47-km paved route built on an old railway line. It offers a smooth, car-free journey through unspoiled, green landscapes, complete with impressive iron railway bridges and cool tunnels, making it ideal for a relaxed and scenic ride.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Centro?
A: The Centro region is generally pleasant for road cycling for much of the year. Spring and autumn offer mild temperatures and beautiful scenery, ideal for longer rides. Coastal areas can be enjoyable even in winter, though mountain routes may experience colder conditions and require appropriate gear. Summers can be hot, so early morning or late afternoon rides are recommended.

Q: Are there routes that pass through charming towns or villages where I can stop for coffee?
A: Many road cycling routes in Centro weave through charming villages and historical towns, providing ample opportunities for coffee stops and to experience local culture. The region is known for its 'schist villages' hidden within mountain slopes, and routes often connect these picturesque spots, allowing for refreshing breaks and exploration.

Q: Are there any routes that explore the unique natural parks of Centro?
A: Yes, Centro is home to several natural parks that offer distinct cycling environments. You can find routes that traverse the Natural Park of Aire and Candeeiros Mountains, known for its karstic formations, or explore the harsh, silent nature of the Tejo Internacional Natural Park. These parks provide unique backdrops for your rides.

Q: Can I find routes that follow rivers or explore coastal areas?
A: Centro offers both river valley and coastal routes. You can cycle along the Ecopista do Dão, which follows the Dão River, or explore routes that hug the Atlantic coastline, offering 'endless Atlantic beaches' and inspiring 'beach hopping' experiences with stunning ocean views. An example is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1153330702/alcala-de-jucar-from-albacete-circular-along-the-banks-of-the-rio-jucar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcalá de Júcar from Albacete — circular along the banks of the Río Júcar&lt;/a&gt;.
tips: Place where the N-322 road crosses the river Júcar. Parallel to the road there is an old bridge if you want to stop - on the national road there can be quite a bit of traffic.
View of an old mill located on the outskirts of the village of Mahora. You can get closer but by a dirt road.

Municipality located on the right bank of the Júcar river. In the Plaza del Ayuntamiento there are some bars if you need to recharge your batteries. On Calle Valencia you can see an old “cuco” (egg-shaped building made of stone and clay).
Excellent 20 km bike lane between Albacete and Valdeganga, perfect surface, parallel to the national highway and very quiet.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the Mahora from Albacete — circular by Valdeganga route?
faqs.answer: This road cycling route is rated as moderate. It covers 69.1 kilometers with an elevation gain of about 320 meters, making it suitable for cyclists with good fitness.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route is characterized by mostly well-paved surfaces, making it ideal for road bikes. A significant portion includes an excellent 20 km dedicated bike lane between Albacete and Valdeganga, known for its perfect surface and quiet nature.

faqs.question: What landmarks or points of interest will I see along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter several interesting features, including the Júcar River, a View of the Mahora windmill, and the historic Cuco of Valdeganga. A notable part of the journey is the dedicated Albacete - Valdeganga bike path.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Albacete region generally offers beautiful and peaceful landscapes for cycling. Spring and autumn are often ideal, providing pleasant temperatures for a moderate road ride without the extreme heat of summer or the chill of winter.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the route?
faqs.answer: As the route starts from Albacete, you would typically find parking within the city. Specific trailhead parking isn't detailed, but public parking options in Albacete would be your starting point for this circular tour.

faqs.question: Are there any places to stop for refreshments or amenities along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, you can find amenities in towns along the route. For example, Mahora has bars in the Plaza del Ayuntamiento where you can stop to recharge.

faqs.question: Is this route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: This is a road cycling route primarily on paved surfaces and dedicated bike lanes. While dogs are generally allowed in public spaces, it's not typically considered a 'dog-friendly trail' in the same way a hiking trail might be. If bringing a dog, ensure it can safely travel in a bike trailer or is accustomed to road cycling environments.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or is there an entrance fee for this cycling route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle the Mahora from Albacete — circular by Valdeganga route. It uses public roads and a dedicated bike path.

faqs.question: Does the route connect with other cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates a significant portion of the Albacete-Valdeganga cycling path. It also briefly intersects with sections of the Circuito Ciclopecuario and the Camino de Santiago de Levante.

faqs.question: What kind of bike is best suited for this route?
faqs.answer: Given the well-paved surfaces and the nature of the route, a racebike (road bike) is the most suitable choice for this tour. Its design is optimized for speed and efficiency on smooth roads.

faqs.question: Is the route suitable for families with children?
faqs.answer: While the route is circular and includes a dedicated bike lane, its moderate difficulty, distance of 69.1 kilometers, and duration of over 3 hours make it more suitable for experienced cyclists. It might be challenging for younger children or families looking for a very leisurely ride.
